_about: Mini cadences: tonic/dominant alternation generated over static harmony using little chords over alternate bass notes. Roman numerals are relative to the prevailing key; 'rh/bass' means RH little chord over LH bass. Examples in B♭ major.

wheel._about: Home/away cycle. Roman numerals refer to the RH little chord. ♭iii°7 and ♯iv°7 are inversions of i°7. Walking clockwise generates I–VI–ii–V-type motion over a static tonic area.

home: im6 (also V6 or I6 colors over i bass)

passingDiminished.i°7: moves both directions; spelled ♭iii°7 leaving home, ♯iv°7 returning

bridgeFormula._about: For a cycle of dominants (e.g. rhythm-changes bridge), alternate the important minor with its same-root diminished over each dominant bass: vm6/X7 → v°7/X7 creates X9 → X7♭9 pulling to the next dominant.

generatorContract: miniCadence(key, mode, bars) returns a sequence of {rhLittleChord, bass, impliedSymbol} alternating home and away states connected by passing chords, never leaving the tonic area unless asked.
